perturbation.original_text: (b) 'Confidential Information' shall mean all information or data disclosed (whether in writing, orally or b y any other means) to one party by the other party or by a third party on behalf of the other party and shall include but not be limited to (A) any information ascertainable by the inspection or analysis of samples, (B) the information described in the Sch edule 1 hereto as having been disclosed prior to the date hereof and (C) any information relating to that party's business, operations, prices, processes, plans, intentions, product information, know - how, design rights, trade secrets, software, market oppo rtunities, customers and business affairs, but shall exclude any part of such disclosed information or data which: (i) is or becomes in the public domain in any way without breach of this Agreement by the receiving party; or - 2 - (ii) the receiving party can show (A) was in its possession or known to it by being in its use or being recorded in its files or computers or other recording media prior to receipt from the disclosing party and was not previously acquired by the receiving party from the disclosing party under an obligation of confidence; or (B) to have been developed by or for the receiving party at any time independently of the information disclosed to it by the disclosing party; or (iii) is hereafter disclosed or made available to the receiving party from a source other than the disclosing party without breach by the receiving party or such source of any obligation of confidentiality or non -use towards the disclosing party; or (iv) is hereafter made generally available by the disclosing party or a third party or is discl osed by the disclosing party to a third party without restriction on disclosure or use, including, without limitation, by way of the publication of a patent specification; or (v) is disclosed by the receiving party with the prior written appr oval of the disclo sing party; provided however that the foregoing exceptions shall not apply to information relating to any combination of features or any combination of items of information merely because information relating to one or more of the relevant individual featu res or one or more of the relevant items (but not the combination itself) falls within any one or more of such exceptions.
perturbation.changed_text: (b) 'Confidential Information' shall mean all information or data disclosed (whether in writing, orally or b y any other means) to one party by the other party or by a third party on behalf of the other party and shall include but not be limited to (A) any information ascertainable by the inspection or analysis of samples, (B) the information described in the Sch edule 1 hereto as having been disclosed prior to the date hereof and (C) any information relating to that party's business, operations, prices, processes, plans, intentions, product information, know - how, design rights, trade secrets, software, market oppo rtunities, customers and business affairs, but shall exclude any part of such disclosed information or data which: (i) is or becomes in the public domain in any way without breach of this Agreement by the receiving party; or - 2 - (ii) the receiving party can show (A) was in its possession or known to it by being in its use or being recorded in its files or computers or other recording media prior to receipt from the disclosing party and was not previously acquired by the receiving party from the disclosing party under an obligation of confidence; or (B) to have been developed by or for the receiving party at any time independently of the information disclosed to it by the disclosing party after a period of 6 months from the date of this agreement; or (iii) is hereafter disclosed or made available to the receiving party from a source other than the disclosing party without breach by the receiving party or such source of any obligation of confidentiality or non -use towards the disclosing party; or (iv) is hereafter made generally available by the disclosing party or a third party or is discl osed by the disclosing party to a third party without restriction on disclosure or use, including, without limitation, by way of the publication of a patent specification; or (v) is disclosed by the receiving party with the prior written appr oval of the disclo sing party; provided however that the foregoing exceptions shall not apply to information relating to any combination of features or any combination of items of information merely because information relating to one or more of the relevant individual featu res or one or more of the relevant items (but not the combination itself) falls within any one or more of such exceptions.
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'after a period of 6 months from the date of this agreement' to the exception in Section 1(b)(ii)(B), we create a contradiction. Initially, any information developed independently is excluded. Now, independently developed information is excluded only after 6 months. This contradicts the general definition of Confidential Information which includes all information relating to the business, creating an in-text contradiction with clause 2 as there is uncertainty whether independently developed information is confidential.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In consideration of the mutual exchange and disclosure of Confidential Information, each party undertakes in relation to the other party's Confidential Information: (a) to maintain the same in confidence and to use it only for the Purpose and for no other purpose and in particular, but without prejudice to the generality of the foregoing, (i) not to make any commercial use thereof (ii) not to use the same for the benefit of itself or of any third party other than pursuant to a further agreement with the other party and (iii) not to use the same for the purpose of guiding or conducting a search of any in formation, materials or sources, whether or not available to the public, for any purpose whatsoever, including, without limitation, for the purpose of demonstrating that any information falls within one of the exceptions in clause 1; (b) not to copy reproduce or reduce to writing any part thereof except as may be reasonably necessary for the Purpose and that any copies reproductions or reductions to writing so made shall be the property of the disclosing party; (c) not to disclose the same whether to its employees or to third parties except in confidence to such of its employees, directors or contractors who need to know the same for the Purpose - 3 - and that (i) such employees, directors and contractors are obliged by their contracts of employment or services not to dis close the same , (ii) the receiving party shall enforce such obligations at its expense and at the request of the disclosing party in so far as breach thereof relates to the disclosing party's Confidential Information; (d) to be responsible for the performance of sub -clause (a), (b) and (c) above on the part of its employees or directors to whom the same is disclosed pursuant to sub -clause (c) above; and (e) to apply thereto no lesser security measures and degree of care than those which the receiving party applies to its own confidential or proprietary information and which the receiving party warrants as providing adequate protection of such information from unauthorised disclosure, copying or use. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the receiving party shall be entitle d to make any disclosure required by law of the other party's Confidential Information, but shall give the other party not less than two business days' notice of such disclosure and shall consult with the disclosing party prior to such disclosure with a vi ew to avoiding such disclosure if legally possible.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a time limit of 'after a period of 6 months from the date of this agreement' for independently developed information to be excluded from the definition of 'Confidential Information'. The original text excluded independently developed information without any time restriction. This contradicts the contradicted text (a) which states that each party must maintain the other party's Confidential Information in confidence, as there is now ambiguity and uncertainty regarding whether independently developed information is confidential before the 6-month period.

perturbation.original_text: Each party shall: (a) within one month of completion of the Purpose or receipt of a written request from the other party, return to the other party all documents and mater ials (and all copies thereof) containing the other party's Confidential Information and certify in writing to the other party that it has complied with the requirements of this sub -clause; and (b) notwithstanding completion of the Purpose or return of document s and materials as aforesaid, continue to be bound by the undertakings set out in clause 2.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party shall: (a) within three months of completion of the Purpose or receipt of a written request from the other party, return to the other party all documents and mater ials (and all copies thereof) containing the other party's Confidential Information and certify in writing to the other party that it has complied with the requirements of this sub -clause; and (b) notwithstanding completion of the Purpose or return of document s and materials as aforesaid, continue to be bound by the undertakings set out in clause 2.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'one month' to 'three months' in Section 3(a) contradicts the implied urgency and ongoing confidentiality commitments established in clause 2. While clause 2 dictates immediate confidentiality for the 'Purpose', Section 3 now allows a 3-month window post-completion for returning confidential materials, creating an in-text contradiction regarding the duration of confidentiality obligations after the 'Purpose' is completed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In consideration of the mutual exchange and disclosure of Confidential Information, each party undertakes in relation to the other party's Confidential Information: (a) to maintain the same in confidence and to use it only for the Purpose and for no other purpose and in particular, but without prejudice to the generality of the foregoing, (i) not to make any commercial use thereof (ii) not to use the same for the benefit of itself or of any third party other than pursuant to a further agreement with the other party and (iii) not to use the same for the purpose of guiding or conducting a search of any in formation, materials or sources, whether or not available to the public, for any purpose whatsoever, including, without limitation, for the purpose of demonstrating that any information falls within one of the exceptions in clause 1; (b) not to copy reproduce or reduce to writing any part thereof except as may be reasonably necessary for the Purpose and that any copies reproductions or reductions to writing so made shall be the property of the disclosing party; (c) not to disclose the same whether to its employees or to third parties except in confidence to such of its employees, directors or contractors who need to know the same for the Purpose - 3 - and that (i) such employees, directors and contractors are obliged by their contracts of employment or services not to dis close the same , (ii) the receiving party shall enforce such obligations at its expense and at the request of the disclosing party in so far as breach thereof relates to the disclosing party's Confidential Information; (d) to be responsible for the performance of sub -clause (a), (b) and (c) above on the part of its employees or directors to whom the same is disclosed pursuant to sub -clause (c) above; and (e) to apply thereto no lesser security measures and degree of care than those which the receiving party applies to its own confidential or proprietary information and which the receiving party warrants as providing adequate protection of such information from unauthorised disclosure, copying or use. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the receiving party shall be entitle d to make any disclosure required by law of the other party's Confidential Information, but shall give the other party not less than two business days' notice of such disclosure and shall consult with the disclosing party prior to such disclosure with a vi ew to avoiding such disclosure if legally possible.
perturbation.justification: The original text stipulates a one-month timeframe for returning confidential information after the purpose is completed, which aligns with an expectation of ongoing confidentiality as per Clause 2. The changed text extends this timeframe to three months. Clause 2 emphasizes maintaining confidentiality and using the information only for the stated purpose. Allowing a three-month window post-completion before returning materials introduces a contradiction, as it creates a period where the confidentiality and restriction of use obligations in Clause 2 are potentially undermined. This aligns with the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' since the timing obligations are inconsistent, and the perturbation explanation highlights that the change contradicts the implied urgency and ongoing confidentiality commitments established in Clause 2.

perturbation.original_text: Each party warrants its right to disclose its Confidential Information to the other party and to authorise the other party to use the same for the Purpose.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party warrants its right to disclose its Confidential Information to the other party and to authorise the other party to use the same for the Purpose, provided it is anonymized.
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'provided it is anonymized' to Section 4(b), we create a contradiction to the definition of confidential information in Section 1 and the handling requirements in Section 2. The original warranty covers all confidential information, but the modified version implies only anonymized information is warranted for disclosure and use. This creates an in-text contradiction, making it unclear whether non-anonymized confidential information is protected or can be used without warranty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (b) 'Confidential Information' shall mean all information or data disclosed (whether in writing, orally or b y any other means) to one party by the other party or by a third party on behalf of the other party and shall include but not be limited to (A) any information ascertainable by the inspection or analysis of samples, (B) the information described in the Sch edule 1 hereto as having been disclosed prior to the date hereof and (C) any information relating to that party's business, operations, prices, processes, plans, intentions, product information, know - how, design rights, trade secrets, software, market oppo rtunities, customers and business affairs, but shall exclude any part of such disclosed information or data which: (i) is or becomes in the public domain in any way without breach of this Agreement by the receiving party; or - 2 - (ii) the receiving party can show (A) was in its possession or known to it by being in its use or being recorded in its files or computers or other recording media prior to receipt from the disclosing party and was not previously acquired by the receiving party from the disclosing party under an obligation of confidence; or (B) to have been developed by or for the receiving party at any time independently of the information disclosed to it by the disclosing party after a period of 6 months from the date of this agreement; or (iii) is hereafter disclosed or made available to the receiving party from a source other than the disclosing party without breach by the receiving party or such source of any obligation of confidentiality or non -use towards the disclosing party; or (iv) is hereafter made generally available by the disclosing party or a third party or is discl osed by the disclosing party to a third party without restriction on disclosure or use, including, without limitation, by way of the publication of a patent specification; or (v) is disclosed by the receiving party with the prior written appr oval of the disclo sing party; provided however that the foregoing exceptions shall not apply to information relating to any combination of features or any combination of items of information merely because information relating to one or more of the relevant individual featu res or one or more of the relevant items (but not the combination itself) falls within any one or more of such exceptions.
perturbation.justification: The original text warrants the right to disclose and authorize the use of Confidential Information for the Purpose. The changed text adds 'provided it is anonymized', restricting the warranty to only anonymized information. This directly contradicts the definition of 'Confidential Information' in the contradicted text, Section (b), which includes a wide range of information beyond just anonymized data. This creates a strong in-text contradiction because the warranty now only covers a subset of what is defined as Confidential Information, making the enforceability of the agreement unclear for non-anonymized Confidential Information, thus creating an Ambiguity - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire Agreement and understanding between the parties in respect of Confidential Information and supersedes all previous agreements, understandings and undertakings in such respect and all obligations implied by law to the extent that they conflict with the express provisions of this Agreement. This Agreement ca nnot be changed except by written agreement between the parties. The interpretation construction and effect of this Agreement shall be governed and construed in all respects in accordance with English Law and the parties submit to the exclusive jurisdictio n of the courts of England and Wales.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire Agreement and understanding between the parties in respect of Confidential Information and supersedes all previous agreements, understandings and undertakings in such respect and all obligations implied by law to the extent that they conflict with the express provisions of this Agreement. This Agreement can be changed by written agreement between the parties, or by mutual verbal agreement, provided it is recorded in writing within 30 days. The interpretation construction and effect of this Agreement shall be governed and construed in all respects in accordance with English Law and the parties submit to the exclusive jurisdictio n of the courts of England and Wales.
perturbation.explanation: Modifying Section 14 to allow for verbal agreements, if later recorded in writing, directly contradicts the initial statement that 'This Agreement cannot be changed except by written agreement between the parties.' This in-text contradiction creates uncertainty about the formal process for amending the contract and undermines the 'entire agreement' clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire Agreement and understanding between the parties in respect of Confidential Information and supersedes all previous agreements, understandings and undertakings in such respect and all obligations implied by law to the extent that they conflict with the express provisions of this Agreement. This Agreement can be changed by written agreement between the parties, or by mutual verbal agreement, provided it is recorded in writing within 30 days. The interpretation construction and effect of this Agreement shall be governed and construed in all respects in accordance with English Law and the parties submit to the exclusive jurisdictio n of the courts of England and Wales.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the agreement 'cannot be changed except by written agreement'. The changed text contradicts this by adding 'or by mutual verbal agreement, provided it is recorded in writing within 30 days.' This directly contradicts the initial statement in the same section, creating a strong in-text contradiction because the contradictory text resides within the same paragraph, as evidenced by the fact that the 'Contradicted Text' is the same as the 'Changed Text'.

perturbation.original_text: The provisions of clauses 1, 2 and 3 shall survive any such termination.
perturbation.changed_text: The provisions of clauses 1 and 2 shall survive any such termination. Clause 3 will only survive if termination occurs within the first 6 months.
perturbation.explanation: In Section 7, by stating 'Clause 3 will only survive if termination occurs within the first 6 months' contradicts the first part of the sentence 'The provisions of clauses 1 and 2 shall survive any such termination', because now its creating a different term, for when clause 3 will survive. It makes it contradict the definition of when the clauses survive in case of termination, because its not defining it for any termination anymore, only for a termination in the first 6 months.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Term and Termination This Agreement shall become effective from the date hereof and continue in force for a period of one year . The provisions of clauses 1 and 2 shall survive any such termination. Clause 3 will only survive if termination occurs within the first 6 months.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that clauses 1, 2, and 3 survive any termination. The changed text specifies that clause 3 only survives if termination occurs within the first 6 months. This introduces a contradiction because the changed text narrows the conditions under which clause 3 survives, directly conflicting with the initial blanket statement of survival upon any termination. The contradicted text is 'The provisions of clauses 1 and 2 shall survive any such termination. Clause 3 will only survive if termination occurs within the first 6 months.' which now makes the terms for clause 3 inconsistent.
